titleStmt.title: Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister Katie Mooney, 6 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Peter Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Peter Mooney was born 5 February 1886 in Folistown, Dunboyne, Co Meath. Orphaned at age 7, he and his sister Katie (b. 1884) were raised by their uncle William and his wife Mary in Caulstown. While there are no surviving enlistment papers for Peter, his War Badge Index Record shows his enlistment date as 18 October 1915.
He served with the Royal Artillery and later transferred to the Royal Engineers where he was promoted to the rank of Sergeant. He served in several places at the Western Front, including Ypres, St. Omer and Passchendael. Peter and Kate were avid correspondents. This letter is from a collection of over ninety surviving letters.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dec 6th 1915 Dear Katie, I forgot to mention
a few things that I want
you to send me on Thursday.
I want some tobbaco &
an Ingorsell Black Watch.
I lost the one I brought
here. Get a Crelloid Case
for it. also a leather
Chain.
I am going on great here
I had a letter from Mr
Martin on Saturday he
sent me the papers to
sign I am sending them
back to day. I am giving
you a lot of trouble with
all you have to send me
I will pray for you all
every day. I now say Good
Bye & God Bless you all. Peter Mooney
